title: alstonia costata
intro: Alstonia costata, also known as the West Indian mast tree, is a species of tree in the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. Alstonia costata has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and acting as an antihistamine, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma. In herbal preparations, Alstonia costata is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections.
